framework.source: inferred: @solidjs/router → solid-router (analogous to @angular/router → angular, keeping package scope qualifier for clarity)

examples.note: Dynamic segment with colon prefix; accessible via useParams().id
Optional segment with ? suffix; matches both /stories and /stories/123
Named wildcard; matched remainder accessible via useParams().any. Wildcard token must appear at end of path.
Catch-all route for 404 handling; named wildcard at root level
matchFilters prop allows regex/array/function validation of segment values; invalid values cause route to not match
Nested routes: only leaf <Route> nodes are real routes. A parent route needs a child '/' route to render at its own path.

clientDetection.value: isServer from 'solid-js/web' (false on client, true on server)

hooks.name: useBeforeLeave
hooks.access: Must be called inside a Solid component tree that is rendered under a <Router>; internally subscribes via RouterContext's beforeLeave lifecycle

availableApi.name: event.from
availableApi.description: The current location (BeforeLeaveEventArgs.from) — location object before navigation

availableApi.name: event.to
availableApi.description: The target path or location being navigated to

availableApi.name: event.retry(force?)
availableApi.description: Re-attempt the blocked navigation, optionally forcing it through even if blocked again

hooks.name: useIsRouting
hooks.access: Must be called inside a Solid component tree under a <Router>; returns a reactive signal

availableApi.name: isRouting()
availableApi.description: Reactive boolean signal; true while a navigation transition is in progress, false when navigation has completed (after startTransition resolves and isRouting is reset)

hooks.name: useLocation
hooks.access: Must be called inside a Solid component tree under a <Router>; returns a reactive location object

availableApi.name: location.state
availableApi.description: History state associated with current entry

availableApi.name: location.key
availableApi.description: Unique key for this history entry

hooks.name: useCurrentMatches
hooks.access: Must be called inside a Solid component tree under a <Router>; returns a reactive accessor returning the array of matched route segments

availableApi.name: matches()
availableApi.description: Array of matched route segments for the current route; each element has route.info, route.path, and params

availableApi.name: matches()[n].route.info
availableApi.description: Custom route info metadata attached to the route definition

hooks.name: useParams
hooks.access: Must be called inside a Solid component rendered as part of a matched route (within the component tree under a <Route>)

availableApi.name: params.<segment>
availableApi.description: Reactive store of current path parameter values keyed by segment name (e.g. params.id for :id)

routingChanges: Re-export context: router context is now re-exported from the package for advanced use cases
Renamed cache() to query() — any code using cache() for route data fetching must migrate to query()
Renamed load to preload — route preload functions must use the preload prop instead of load
Default form actions now use URL encoding (application/x-www-form-urlencoded) instead of multipart
Response helpers (redirect, reload) now return Response objects; cache/action filter them out of render
Added createAsyncStorage; added prev argument to cache callbacks; removed store from cache API
Initial changesets adoption; no routing API breaking changes documented

structured_output.notes:
1. Route definition: @solidjs/router supports both JSX-declarative style (<Route> components as children of <Router>) and config-object style (array of {path, component} objects passed as children). Both are equivalent at runtime — there is no true 'file-based' routing in the framework itself.
2. No post-navigation hook: There is no 'afterRouteChange' or equivalent event. The closest approximation is a reactive effect on useLocation() or watching useIsRouting() transition from true to false. These run after route matching and transition commit but before data loading / rendering of new components.
3. Route components as leaves only: Parent <Route> elements without a component do not render anything themselves; only leaf route nodes render components. Nested routes compose via props.children passed down, not via <Outlet> (removed in v0.10.0).
4. matchFilters: Route segments can be validated with the matchFilters prop (accepts arrays for enum matching, RegExp, or predicate functions). A segment failing its filter causes the route not to match.
5. Multiple paths: A single <Route> can accept an array as its path prop; switching between matched paths does not re-mount the component.
6. SSR: Pass url prop to <Router> on server (e.g. <Router url={req.url}>). Use isServer from solid-js/web to branch client/server code.
